title: गोविन्दाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं नित्यमनाकाशं परमाकाशम्।
गोष्ठप्राङ्गणरिङ्खणलोलमनायासं परमायासम्।
मायाकल्पितनानाकारमनाकारं भुवनाकारम्।
क्ष्माया नाथमनाथं प्रणमत गोविन्दं परमानन्दम्॥१॥

मृत्स्नामत्सीहेति यशोदाताडनशैशवसन्त्रासम्।
व्यादितवक्त्रालोकितलोकालोकचतुर्दशलोकालिम्।
लोकत्रयपुरमूलस्तम्भं लोकालोकमनालोकम्।
लोकेशं परमेशं प्रणमत गोविन्दं परमानन्दम्॥२॥

त्रैविष्टपरिपुवीरघ्नं क्षितिभारघ्नं भवरोगघ्नम्।
कैवल्यं नवनीताहारमनाहारं भुवनाहारम्।
वैमल्यस्फुटचेतोवृत्तिविशेषाभासमनाभासम्।
वैशाखस्य परं दैवतमिह प्रणमत गोविन्दं परमानन्दम्॥३॥

गोपालं प्रभुलीलाविग्रहगोपालं कुलगोपालम्।
गोपीखेलनगोवर्धनधृतिलीलालालितगोपालम्।
गोभिर्निगदितगोविन्दस्फुटनामानं बहुनामानम्।
गोपीगोचरदूरं प्रणमत गोविन्दं परमानन्दम्॥४॥

गोपीमण्डलगोष्ठीभेदं भेदावस्थमभेदाभम्।
शश्वद्गोखुरनिर्धूतोद्गतधूलीधूसरसौभाग्यम्।
श्रद्धाभक्तिगृहीतानन्दमचिन्त्यं चिन्तितसद्भावम्।
चिन्तामणिमहिमानं प्रणमत गोविन्दं परमानन्दम्॥५॥

स्नानव्याकुलयोषिद्वस्त्रमुपादायागमुपारूढम्।
व्यादित्सन्तीरथ दिग्वस्त्रा दातुमुपाकर्षद्वेणुम्।
निर्धूतद्वयशोकविमोहं बुद्धं बुद्धेरन्तस्थम्।
सत्ताभासकमानन्दं प्रणमत गोविन्दं परमानन्दम्॥६॥

कान्तं कारणकारणमादिमनादिं कालधनाभासम्।
कालिन्दीगतकालियशिरसि सुनृत्यन्तं मुहुरत्यन्तम्।
कालं कालकलातीतं कलिताशेषं कलिदोषघ्नम्।
कालत्रयगतिहेतुं प्रणमत गोविन्दं परमानन्दम्॥७॥

वृन्दावनभुवि वृन्दारकगणवृन्दाराधितवन्द्यायाम्।
कुन्दाभामलमन्दस्मेरसुधानन्दं सुहृदानन्दम्।
वन्द्याशेषमहामुनिमानसमन्दिरमानन्दं परमानन्दम्।
न्दनन्दनमानन्दं प्रणमत गोविन्दं परमानन्दम्॥८॥

गोविन्दाष्टकमेतदधीते गोविन्दार्पितचेता यः।
गोविन्दाच्युत माधव विष्णो गोकुलनायक कृष्णेति।
गोविन्दाङ्घ्रिसरोजध्यानसुधाजलधौतसमस्ताघो।
गोविन्दं परमानन्दामृतमन्तस्थं स तमभ्येति॥
transliteration:
Satyam Jnanam Anantam Nityam Anakasham Paramakasham.
Goshtha-Prangana-Rinkhana-Lolam Anayasam Paramayasam.
Maya-Kalpita-Nana-Akaram Anakaram Bhuvanakaram.
Kshmaya Natham Anatham Pranamata Govindam Paramanandam.||1||

Mritsna-Matsi-Heti Yashoda-Tadana-Shaishava-Santrasam.
Vyadita-Vaktra-Alokita-Loka-Aloka-Chaturdasha-Lokalim.
Loka-Traya-Pura-Mula-Stambham Loka-Alokam Analokam.
Lokesham Parameshaam Pranamata Govindam Paramanandam.||2||

Traivishtapa-Ripu-Viraghanam Kshiti-Bharaghnam Bhava-Rogaghnam.
Kaivalyam Navanita-Aharam Anaharam Bhuvana-Aharam.
Vaimalya-Sphuta-Cheto-Vritti-Vishesha-Abhasam Anabhasam.
Vaishakhasya Param Daivatam Iha Pranamata Govindam Paramanandam.||3||

Gopalam Prabhu-Lila-Vigraha-Gopalam Kula-Gopalam.
Gopi-Khelana-Govardhana-Dhriti-Lila-Lalita-Gopalam.
Gobhir-Nigadita-Govinda-Sphuta-Namanam Bahu-Namanam.
Gopi-Gochara-Duram Pranamata Govindam Paramanandam.||4||

Gopi-Mandala-Goshthi-Bhedam Bheda-Avastham Abheda-Abham.
Shashvad-Go-Khura-Nirdhuta-Udgata-Dhuli-Dhusara-Saubhagyam.
Shraddha-Bhakti-Grihita-Anandam Achintyam Chintita-Sadbhavam.
Chintamani-Mahimanam Pranamata Govindam Paramanandam.||5||

Snana-Vyakula-Yoshid-Vastram Upadaya-Agam-Uparudham.
Vyaditsantir-Atha Dig-Vastra Datum-Upakarshad-Venum.
Nirdhuta-Dvaya-Shoka-Vimoham Buddham Buddher-Antastham.
Satta-Abhasakam Anandam Pranamata Govindam Paramanandam.||6||

Kantam Karana-Karanam Adim Anadim Kala-Dhana-Abhasam.
Kalindi-Gata-Kaliya-Shirasi Sunrityantam Muhur-Atyantam.
Kalam Kala-Kala-Atitam Kalita-Ashesham Kali-Dosha-Ghnam.
Kala-Traya-Gati-Hetum Pranamata Govindam Paramanandam.||7||

Vrindavana-Bhuvi Vrindara-Gana-Vrinda-Aradhita-Vandyayam.
Kunda-Abha-Amala-Manda-Smera-Sudha-Anandam Suhrid-Anandam.
Vandya-Ashesha-Maha-Muni-Manasa-Mandiram Anandam Paramanandam.
Nanda-Nandanam Anandam Pranamata Govindam Paramanandam.||8||

Govinda-Ashtakam-Etad-Adhite Govinda-Arpita-Cheta Yah.
Govinda-Achyuta Madhava Vishno Gokula-Nayaka Krishneti.
Govinda-Anghri-Saroja-Dhyana-Sudha-Jaladhauta-Samasta-Agho.
Govindam Paramananda-Amritam Antastham Sa Tam Abhyeti.
hindiMeaning:
जो सत्य, ज्ञान और अनन्त हैं, जो नित्य हैं, जो आकाश से परे परम आकाश हैं, जो गोकुल के आँगन में बालसुलभ लीलाएँ करते हुए विचरण करते हैं, जो अनायास परम प्रयास करने वाले हैं, जो माया द्वारा कल्पित अनेक रूपों में प्रकट होते हुए भी निराकार और भुवनाकार हैं, जो पृथ्वी के नाथ होते हुए भी अनाथ हैं - उन परमानन्द स्वरूप गोविन्द को प्रणाम करो।

जो बालक अवस्था में माता यशोदा के मिट्टी खाने पर डाँटने से भयभीत हो गए, परन्तु मुख खोलने पर जिनके मुख में चौदह लोक दिखाई दिए, जो तीनों लोकों के मूल स्तम्भ हैं - उन लोकेश्वर, परमेश्वर, परमानन्द स्वरूप गोविन्द को प्रणाम करो।
benefits: Deepens devotion and connection with Lord Krishna
Invokes spiritual wisdom and inner peace
Traditionally recited for removal of worldly attachments and illusions
Invokes the blessing of supreme bliss and divine grace
Purifies the mind and heart of the devotee
description: Govinda Ashtakam (गोविन्दाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn dedicated to Lord Krishna. This revered text originates from Shankaracharya. Devotees recite this prayer to remove worldly attachments and illusions and lead towards liberation and self-realization. It is also said to bestow supreme bliss and divine grace. For best results, devotees chant this on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 10 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Govinda Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Govinda Ashtakam in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Eight verses glorifying Lord Govinda for supreme bliss and devotion.

seoFaqs.question: Is Govinda Ashtakam the same as Bhaja Govindam by Adi Shankaracharya?
seoFaqs.answer: No. Both texts are attributed to Adi Shankaracharya and both invoke Govinda, but they are distinct compositions. Bhaja Govindam (also called Mohamudgara) is a longer didactic poem, traditionally thirty-one verses, urging the listener to seek Govinda rather than worldly attachments and famously composed when Shankara saw an old grammarian rote-learning rules near Kashi. Govinda Ashtakam is a strictly eight-verse devotional hymn (ashtakam means a poem of eight verses) celebrating Krishna in his loving Vrindavan and cosmic forms. Devotees often chant Govinda Ashtakam as praise and Bhaja Govindam as instruction, treating them as complementary works in the Shankara stotra corpus.

seoFaqs.question: Why does the hymn use the name Govinda for Krishna?
seoFaqs.answer: Govinda literally means cowherd or one who pleases the cows and the earth (go can mean cow, earth, or sense organ). The name was bestowed on Krishna when he lifted Mount Govardhana to shelter the cattle and cowherds of Vrindavan from Indra's storm, an episode the ashtakam alludes to in its imagery. The name also features prominently in the Vishnu Sahasranama and in the Govinda Damodara Madhava chant. By choosing Govinda over other Krishna names, Shankara emphasises the deity's intimate, pastoral, and protective face rather than the regal warrior of the Mahabharata, setting the devotional tone of all eight verses.

seoFaqs.question: What aspects of Krishna's life does Govinda Ashtakam describe?
seoFaqs.answer: Each of the eight verses paints a different scene from Krishna's leelas. The opening verse invokes him as the all-pervading sustainer of the universe. Subsequent verses describe him as the toddler who crawled in Yashoda's courtyard, the boy who stole butter from gopis, the cowherd who played the flute under kadamba trees, the lifter of Govardhana who sheltered Vrindavan from Indra's wrath, the slayer of demons such as Aghasura and Kaliya, and the dark-complexioned beloved of Radha and the gopis. The closing verses fold these intimate scenes back into the cosmic vision of Krishna as the unchanging Brahman, the same reality that wears the peacock feather.

seoFaqs.question: When and how is Govinda Ashtakam traditionally chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The ashtakam is chanted year-round, but its recitation intensifies during Janmashtami (Krishna's birth), Govardhan Puja (the day after Diwali, marking Krishna's lifting of the hill), and through the Karthika and Margashirsha months that are dear to Vaishnavas. Devotees often chant it in front of a Krishna icon decorated with tulsi leaves and a small lamp of ghee. Many begin with a sankalpa (intention), recite the eight verses three or eleven times, and conclude with a few minutes of silent dhyana on the form just praised. Because the verses are short and lyrical, the ashtakam is also a popular choice for children learning Sanskrit recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What does the closing phala-shruti verse promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Like most ashtakams in the Shankara tradition, Govinda Ashtakam ends with a phala-shruti (declaration of fruits) that summarises the benefits of regular chanting. The verse promises that whoever sings these eight stanzas glorifying Govinda with sincere devotion easily crosses the ocean of samsara, the cycle of birth and death. The fruit named is therefore not material reward but moksha or liberation, in keeping with Adi Shankara's Advaita orientation. Devotees who chant for worldly purposes such as health or harmony are not discouraged, but the text itself foregrounds liberation as its highest gift.

seoFaqs.question: How does Govinda Ashtakam fit within Adi Shankara's wider stotra corpus?
seoFaqs.answer: Adi Shankara composed many short ashtakams praising specific deities, including Krishna Ashtakam, Achyuta Ashtakam, Shiva Panchakshara Stotram, Annapurna Ashtakam and Bhavani Ashtakam, all designed to make devotional access easy alongside his more demanding philosophical works. Govinda Ashtakam belongs to this devotional layer of his oeuvre, where the strict non-dualism of his commentaries is expressed through warmly personal praise of saguna forms. Reading Govinda Ashtakam alongside Krishna Ashtakam and Achyuta Ashtakam gives a fuller picture of how Shankara approached Krishna devotion, weaving lila-rich imagery into Vedantic conviction.
